title: Podman containerfile order breaks `podman build` (WORKDIR, VOLUME)
body:
### Issue Description

Good day!

this might be a duplicate, but I wasn't able to find a clear similar issue so forgive me if this is already logged. (Or maybe not a bug but a feature ? ;-) )

Given a `Dockerfile`:

```Dockerfile
FROM debian

VOLUME /home/bla
WORKDIR /home/bla
RUN echo "printf 'hello world'" | tee ./runme.sh
RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt
```

If i build it using `docker` it works okay:

```
svanellewee:~/source/container-study $ docker build --no-cache . -t test4
[+] Building 0.6s (8/8) FINISHED                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 172B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/debian:latest                                                                                                                                                                                                                     0.0s
 => [1/4] FROM docker.io/library/debian                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              0.0s
 => CACHED [2/4] WORKDIR /home/bla                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   0.0s
 => [3/4] RUN echo "printf 'hello world'" | tee ./runme.sh                                                                                                                                                                                                                           0.2s
 => [4/4] RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           0.4s
 => exporting to image                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               0.0s
 => => exporting layers                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              0.0s
 => => writing image sha256:0740ed21054a511b1333af18e15e8b6c16f359cc6e468d0795dfc8dcad1839df                                                                                                                                                                                         0.0s
 => => naming to docker.io/library/test4                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             0.0s
```

Running the same on podman seems to break this build. (I built this locally from source, hence the odd path)

```
svanellewee:~/source/container-study $ ~/source/podman/bin/podman build --no-cache . -t test4
STEP 1/5: FROM debian
STEP 2/5: VOLUME /home/bla
--> 5ad5b2678bb0
STEP 3/5: WORKDIR /home/bla
--> 514df69ce712
STEP 4/5: RUN echo "printf 'hello world'" | tee ./runme.sh
printf 'hello world'
--> ec0909356c34
STEP 5/5: RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt
bash: ./runme.sh: No such file or directory
Error: building at STEP "RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt": while running runtime: exit status 127
svanellewee:~/source/container-study $ 
```
Any info here would be appreciated! I admit it's an contrived example, but summarizes an issue I've noticed in my day to day.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. build the container using podman

### Describe the results you received

The file I created in a build step no longer exists and fails with "No such file or directory"

### Describe the results you expected

I expected the `docker build` and `podman build` behaviour to match.

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Running the test on Ubuntu Linux 
Kernel 5.19.0-41-generic containers/podman#42~22.04.1-Ubuntu 
Docker is standard apt-installed docker as per the website. (rootful)

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
having the same issue, with docker works, but with buildah not
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

The observed `docker build` behavior might be a BuildKit-ism? 

The Dockerfile reference mentions the following for the VOLUME statement (https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#volume):

> ### Notes about specifying volumes
> ...
> * **Changing the volume from within the Dockerfile**: If any build steps change the data within the volume after it has been declared, those changes will be discarded.

This aligns with the observed `podman build` behavior.

However, when BuildKit is used, the VOLUME statement doesn't seem to exhibit those semantics:

```
$ docker version
Client:
 Version:           23.0.6+azure-2
...
Server:
 Engine:
  Version:          23.0.6+azure-2
...
$ DOCKER_BUILDKIT=1 docker build --no-cache . -t test4
[+] Building 2.5s (8/8) FINISHED                                                           docker:default
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                 0.1s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 169B                                                                 0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                    0.2s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                      0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/debian:latest                                     0.0s
 => [1/4] FROM docker.io/library/debian                                                              0.0s
 => CACHED [2/4] WORKDIR /home/bla                                                                   0.0s
 => [3/4] RUN echo "printf 'hello world'" | tee ./runme.sh                                           0.8s
 => [4/4] RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt                                                           0.6s
 => exporting to image                                                                               0.9s
 => => exporting layers                                                                              0.9s
 => => writing image sha256:1e103f5c7feef3650c64e8358616de7cf06484447cd62dc3a011312acdc1dde7         0.0s
 => => naming to docker.io/library/test4                                                             0.0s
```

When BuildKit is disabled (i.e., the legacy builder is used), `docker build` exhibits the same behavior as `podman build`: 

```
$ DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0 docker build --no-cache . -t test4
DEPRECATED: The legacy builder is deprecated and will be removed in a future release.
            BuildKit is currently disabled; enable it by removing the DOCKER_BUILDKIT=0
            environment-variable.

Sending build context to Docker daemon  7.114MB
Step 1/5 : FROM debian
 ---> 3a37950934ff
Step 2/5 : VOLUME /home/bla
 ---> Running in 1f3ebe11aa01
Removing intermediate container 1f3ebe11aa01
 ---> 86642020935f
Step 3/5 : WORKDIR /home/bla
 ---> Running in 6255221c3fad
Removing intermediate container 6255221c3fad
 ---> be797d25812e
Step 4/5 : RUN echo "printf 'hello world'" | tee ./runme.sh
 ---> Running in db7d4d57e5d6
printf 'hello world'
Removing intermediate container db7d4d57e5d6
 ---> 1b15b4200701
Step 5/5 : RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt
 ---> Running in 990bf7306f1f
bash: ./runme.sh: No such file or directory
The command '/bin/sh -c bash ./runme.sh > output.txt' returned a non-zero code: 127
```
Oh wow! It didn't occur to me that Buildkit would cause that. Sorry everyone!

Tested it again, stealing from [this article](https://pythonspeed.com/articles/podman-buildkit/)

```
svanellewee:~/source/container-study $ ~/source/podman/bin/podman run -d --name buildkitd --privileged docker.io/moby/buildkit:latest                                                                       
03f71de25c81cb5d0f8f878b706ece4c880274c959778349c1928d00f8df0b12    
```

Then ran the build:

```
svanellewee:~/source/container-study $ buildctl --addr=podman-container://buildkitd build \                                                                                                                 
   --frontend dockerfile.v0 \                                                                                                                                                                               
   --local context=. \                                                                                                                                                                                      
   --local dockerfile=. \                                                                                                                                                                                   
   --export-cache type=inline \                                                                                                                                                                             
   --output type=docker,name=mynewimage | podman load                                                                                                                                                       
[+] Building 33.2s (9/9) FINISHED                                                                                                                                                                           
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                                                                   0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 172B                                                                                                                                                                   0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/debian:latest                                                                                                                                       1.9s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                                                                      0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                                                                        0.0s
 => [1/4] FROM docker.io/library/debian:latest@sha256:b91baba9c2cae5edbe3b0ff50ae8f05157e3ae6f018372dcfc3aba224acb392b                                                                                30.7s
 => => resolve docker.io/library/debian:latest@sha256:b91baba9c2cae5edbe3b0ff50ae8f05157e3ae6f018372dcfc3aba224acb392b                                                                                 0.0s
 => => sha256:de4cac68b6165c40cf6f8b30417948c31be03a968e233e55ee40221553a5e570 49.56MB / 49.56MB                                                                                                      30.0s
 => => extracting sha256:de4cac68b6165c40cf6f8b30417948c31be03a968e233e55ee40221553a5e570                                                                                                              0.7s
 => [2/4] WORKDIR /home/bla                                                                                                                                                                            0.1s
 => [3/4] RUN echo "printf 'hello world'" | tee ./runme.sh                                                                                                                                             0.1s
 => [4/4] RUN bash ./runme.sh > output.txt                                                                                                                                                             0.1s
 => preparing layers for inline cache                                                                                                                                                                  0.1s
 => exporting to docker image format                                                                                                                                                                   0.2s
 => => exporting layers                                                                                                                                                                                0.0s
 => => exporting manifest sha256:ecf4fc7c7436e3d3a513ea31b4baf1bdf8b07108b64e943a44c496ec1cb34276                                                                                                      0.0s
 => => exporting config sha256:628186ad3a5ad46c687ed5e740760413026425178c70ef1ce7736d9b9df5ee90                                                                                                        0.0s
 => => sending tarball                                                                                                                                                                                 0.2s
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob de4cac68b616 done  
Copying blob 0df9b06d0685 done  
Copying blob 6a886c5c1d14 done  
Copying blob 25d2025db7d0 done  
Copying config 628186ad3a done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image: docker.io/library/mynewimage:latest
```
and it checks out:

```

svanellewee:~/source/container-study $ ~/source/podman/bin/podman run -it docker.io/library/mynewimage  bash
root@ec3a88010680:/home/bla# ls
output.txt  runme.sh
root@ec3a88010680:/home/bla# cat output.txt 
hello worldroot@ec3a88010680:/home/bla# cat runme.sh 
printf 'hello world'
root@ec3a88010680:/home/bla# ^C
root@ec3a88010680:/home/bla# 
exit
```

So sorry! It completely slipped my mind that buildkit might be the factor. We can close if everyone is okay with this.

An explanation/confirmation here:
https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/37176#issuecomment-470951814

> BuildKit has a slightly different behaviour, where VOLUME statements are not applied during build, so it will actually modify the files that were originally in the given location, and only attach a volume when the container is run.

Additional discussion regarding the difference between RUN and COPY semantics (in the legacy builder):
https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/33842
